# criblio_subscription (Resource)

Subscription Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_subscription" "my_subscription" {
  description = "Subscription for ingesting syslog data"
  group_id = "default"
  id = "my-subscription"
  pipeline = "main"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group_id` (String) Worker group ID.
- `id` (String)
- `pipeline` (String)

### Optional

- `consumer` (Attributes) Consumers are now defined on projects instead of subscriptions. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--consumer))
- `description` (String)
- `disabled` (Boolean)
- `filter` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--consumer"></a>
### Nested Schema for `consumer`

Optional:

- `connections` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--consumer--connections))
- `disabled` (Boolean) It should be removed as a consumer is present or absent.
- `type` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--consumer--connections"></a>
### Nested Schema for `consumer.connections`

Required:

- `output` (String)

Optional:

- `pipeline` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_subscription.my_criblio_subscription
  id = jsonencode({
    group_id = "myExistingGroupId"
    id       = "myUniqueSubscriptionId"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_subscription.my_criblio_subscription '{"group_id": "myExistingGroupId", "id": "myUniqueSubscriptionId"}'
```
